# Scan Bitbucket for secrets

Connect Bitbucket as a data source to HCP Vault Radar to scan your Bitbucket
workspaces and projects for sensitive data and secrets. Vault Radar supports
scanning Bitbucket Cloud and Bitbucket Server.

@include 'vault-radar/radar-quickstart-tutorials.mdx'

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

## Prerequisites

- HCP IAM user with the HCP owner, or admin role
- [Vault Radar agent](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) deployed and running (agent scan only)
- For Bitbucket Cloud: [workspace
  ID](https://support.atlassian.com/bitbucket-cloud/docs/what-is-a-workspace/),
  token creator email address, and [API token with the following
  scopes](https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-account/docs/manage-api-tokens-for-your-atlassian-account/):

  <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

  ```plaintext
  read:account
  read:me
  read:project:bitbucket
  read:pullrequest:bitbucket
  read:repository:bitbucket
  read:user:bitbucket
  read:webhook:bitbucket
  read:workspace:bitbucket
  write:pullrequest:bitbucket
  write:webhook:bitbucket
  ```

  </CodeBlockConfig>

- For Bitbucket Server: project key, account name, and HTTP access token with
  project and repository admin permissions.

## Add a Bitbucket data source

You can add and scan Bitbucket Cloud or Bitbucket Server using the **HCP
Vault Radar cloud scan** or **HCP Vault Radar agent scan**.

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Bitbucket Cloud">

Add Bitbucket Cloud as a data source to scan your Bitbucket organization's repositories for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **Bitbucket Cloud**.

1. Enter the email address of the Bitbucket Cloud user that created the API
   token in the **Enter your Bitbucket token creator email** text field.

1. Enter the API token in the **Enter your Bitbucket API token** field.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Before you configure HCP Vault Radar, export an environment variable on your
   agent in the format of `BITBUCKET_TOKEN=myemail@example.com:mytoken` where
   `myemail@example.com` is the email address of the Bitbucket Cloud user that
   created the API token and `mytoken` is the API token value.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export BITBUCKET_TOKEN=myemail@example.com:mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **Bitbucket Cloud**.

1. Enter the path to the environment variable with the API token in
   the **Enter environment path to your Bitbucket API token**
   field.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://BITBUCKET_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Bitbucket Server">

Add Bitbucket Server as a data source to scan your Bitbucket organization's repositories for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **Bitbucket Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your Bitbucket domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your Bitbucket Server.
   - **Enter your Bitbucket account:** Account name of the Bitbucket Server user
     that created the HTTP access token.
   - **Enter your Bitbucket HTTP access token:** Access token from your Bitbucket Server.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Before you configure HCP Vault Radar, export an environment variable on your
   agent in the format of `BITBUCKET_TOKEN=myemail@example.com:mytoken` where
   `myemail@example.com` is the email address of the Bitbucket Cloud user that
   created the API token and `mytoken` is the API token value.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export BITBUCKET_TOKEN=myemail@example.com:mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **Bitbucket Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your Bitbucket domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your Bitbucket Server.
   - **Enter environment path to your Bitbucket HTTP access token:** Access token from your Bitbucket Server.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://BITBUCKET_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
</Tabs>
